title: 013-012: Wire METHOD:CANCEL invitations on event delete, add deduplication guard
description: In src/App.tsx handleDeleteEvent: capture event before deletion, call sendInvitations(deletedEvent, members, 'CANCEL', sequence+1). For scope='this' recurring deletes pass RECURRENCE-ID. In useSendInvitations add dedup guard: Map<eventId, lastSentTimestamp>; skip if same eventId called within 3000ms. Depends on 013-006,013-011.

title: 013-011: Wire invitation sending on event create and update in App.tsx
description: In src/App.tsx call useSendInvitations(). In handleSaveEvent: after persisting event call sendInvitations(event, members, 'REQUEST', sequence). Add sequence?: number to CalendarEvent type; increment on edit. Handle DeliveryResult[]: toast for skipped (no email), error toast for failed (with retry), brief success toast for sent. Add onUpdateMember handler, pass to FamilyMembersSheet. Depends on 013-006,013-008,013-009.

title: 013-006: Implement useSendInvitations hook — SMTP/mailto dispatch with debounce
description: Create src/hooks/use-send-invitations.ts. Reads smtpConfig and organizerConfig from localStorage. Exposes sendInvitations(event, members, method, sequence): Promise<DeliveryResult[]>. If SMTP configured: POST to /api/send-invitation per recipient. If no SMTP: call buildMailtoLink, open via window.open. 1500ms debounce via useRef. Skipped members (no email) returned with status:'skipped'. Depends on 013-004,013-005.

title: 013-004: Create invitation builder and mailto fallback in invitations.ts
description: Create src/lib/invitations.ts exporting: buildInvitations(event, members, organizerEmail, method, sequence, organizerName): Invitation[] (skip no-email members, skip organizer); buildMailtoLink(invitation, gcalUrl?): string (mailto URI with .ics content in body + instructions, GCal URL if provided, URL-encoded); getMembersWithoutEmail(event, members): FamilyMember[]. Depends on 013-001,013-002.

title: 013-002: Add iMIP single-event ICS generator to ical.ts
description: In src/lib/ical.ts add generateInvitationIcs(event, members, organizerName, organizerEmail, method: 'REQUEST'|'CANCEL', sequence): string. Must include METHOD header, single VEVENT with UID, SEQUENCE, ORGANIZER, ATTENDEE lines per member with email, RRULE for recurring, RECURRENCE-ID for instance edits, STATUS:CANCELLED for METHOD:CANCEL, RFC 5545 line folding. Depends on 013-001.

title: 006: Testing Framework & TDD Standards (Vitest + RTL)
description: Install Vitest and React Testing Library. Add coverage gates (70% lines, 60% branches for src/lib/). Establish TDD standard.\nSpec: specs/006-testing-framework-tdd/spec.md

title: 005: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
description: Automated CI workflow for build, lint, and test on every PR. Deploy workflow on main branch merges.\nSpec: specs/005-github-actions-cicd/spec.md

title: 004: Node.js Version Pinning & Upgrade to v22 LTS
description: Pin project to Node 22 LTS via .nvmrc and package.json engines field. Node 20 is EOL April 2026.\nSpec: specs/004-node-version-upgrade/spec.md

title: 003: Replace GitHub Spark KV with localStorage Backend
description: Remove @github/spark useKV dependency and replace with typed localStorage persistence layer. All persistence goes through a centralized utility module.\nSpec: specs/003-local-storage-backend/spec.md

title: Phase 3: Calendar grid integration for subscription events (US1-P1)
description: Update CalendarGrid.tsx to accept subscribedEvents prop. Render subscription events with their assigned color and a visual indicator (italic/badge) distinguishing them from family events. Add subscription filter chips to App header alongside family member chips.

title: Phase 2: Subscription management UI (US1-P1)
description: Build IcsSubscriptionsSheet.tsx (shadcn Sheet) with form for adding ICS URL/name/color, subscription list, remove button, fetch error badge. Wire useKV<IcsSubscription[]> in App.tsx. Fetch and parse events on mount and on save. Acceptance: user can add a public ICS URL and see its events on the calendar grid.

title: Phase 1: ICS data types and utility library
description: Add IcsSubscription and SubscribedEvent types to src/lib/types.ts. Create src/lib/ics.ts with: fetchAndParseIcs(url, corsProxy), mergeAndStripAlarms(), exportToIcsString(). Add ical.js dependency. This is the foundation required before any UI work.

title: 013-009: Show no-email warnings in EventDialog for assigned members
description: Update src/components/EventDialog.tsx. After member selector, if any selected member has no email: show amber Alert 'Name1, Name2 have no email address — no invitation will be sent'. Add small amber dot indicator on member toggle buttons for email-less members. Advisory only — does not block save. Depends on 013-001,013-007.

title: 013-010: Add 'Add to Google Calendar' button to EventDetailsDialog
description: Update src/components/EventDetailsDialog.tsx. Import buildGCalUrl from src/lib/gcal.ts. Add 'Add to Google Calendar' button (outline, small) that opens buildGCalUrl(event) in new tab. Always visible. Show 'GCal link included in invitation' badge beside GCal-preference members. Depends on 013-003.

title: 013-007: Add delivery preferences UI to FamilyMembersSheet
description: Update src/components/FamilyMembersSheet.tsx. Add email (type=email) and preferredPlatform (select: none/google/apple/outlook/other) fields to the Add Member form. For existing members show their email below name + inline edit form triggered by edit icon. Amber warning icon for members without email. Add onUpdateMember prop and wire up. Depends on 013-001.

title: 013-008: Create SMTP and Organizer settings panel
description: Create src/components/SmtpSettingsPanel.tsx inside FamilyMembersSheet as a collapsible Accordion section. Fields: host, port, secure checkbox, username, password (with show/hide), fromAddress, fromName, organizerName, organizerEmail. Save button (persists to localStorage), Test Connection button (POST to /api/test-smtp, show toast). Status badge: 'SMTP configured' (green) or 'Using mailto fallback' (amber). Depends on 013-001.

title: 013-005: Create serverless SMTP send endpoint
description: Create api/send-invitation.ts as Vercel serverless function. Install nodemailer. Accepts POST { invitation: Invitation, smtpConfig: SmtpConfig }. Creates transporter, sends email with .ics attachment (contentType: text/calendar; method=REQUEST), HTML body with GCal link if present. Also create api/test-smtp.ts for connection verify. Returns { status: 'sent' } or { status: 'failed', error }. Depends on 013-001.

title: 013-003: Build Google Calendar deep-link URL generator
description: Create src/lib/gcal.ts exporting buildGCalUrl(event): string with action=TEMPLATE, text, dates (datetime for timed events, date-only for all-day), details. Also export isGCalUser(member): boolean. Depends on 013-001.

title: 013-001: Extend types and storage keys for delivery preferences
description: In src/lib/types.ts add optional email?: string and preferredPlatform?: 'google'|'apple'|'outlook'|'other' to FamilyMember. Add SmtpConfig, OrganizerConfig, Invitation, and DeliveryResult interfaces. In src/lib/storage.ts add smtpConfig and organizerConfig keys. All new fields optional so existing data round-trips correctly.

title: 012-013: Wire KioskPage into /kiosk route
description: Replace placeholder in router with real KioskPage from 012-012. Verify / route unaffected. No shared state leaks between routes. Depends on 012-012.

title: 012-012: Assemble KioskPage — full viewport layout
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskPage.tsx assembling all kiosk components. Uses useKioskConfig, useKioskData, useLiveClock, useKioskRefresh. Full-viewport layout (w-screen h-screen overflow-hidden), no nav/header/action buttons. Landscape two-panel: left 55% clock+today, right 45% upcoming. KioskSyncWarning absolute bottom-right. Day-change triggers refresh. Depends on 012-006,012-007,012-009,012-010,012-011.

title: 012-010: Build KioskUpcomingPanel component
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskUpcomingPanel.tsx. Props: events: KioskEvent[], maxPerDay=3. Groups next 7 days; day headers; per-day overflow badge; empty state 'Nothing coming up this week'. Scrollable container. Depends on 012-008.

title: 012-009: Build KioskTodayPanel component
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskTodayPanel.tsx. Props: events: KioskEvent[], maxVisible=5. Empty state: 'Nothing scheduled today' with CalendarBlank icon. Overflow: show first maxVisible + '+ N more' badge. All text >=18px. Depends on 012-008.

title: 012-007: Build KioskClockHeader component
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskClockHeader.tsx. Props: now: Date. Shows day-of-week + full date (>=24px) and HH:MM live clock (>=32px). No interactive elements. Tailwind-styled. Depends on 012-001.

title: 012-008: Build KioskEventItem component
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskEventItem.tsx. Props: event: KioskEvent, size: 'large'|'small'. Color-coded left border, title with truncation + Tooltip for full text, optional time range. Large: title>=20px, time>=16px. Small: title>=16px, time>=13px. Depends on 012-002.

title: 012-006: Create useKioskRefresh hook — auto-refresh + Page Visibility API
description: Create src/hooks/use-kiosk-refresh.ts accepting intervalMs and onRefresh callback. Implements setInterval(onRefresh, intervalMs) and visibilitychange listener (calls onRefresh when tab becomes visible). Returns { lastRefreshedAt: Date }. Callback stable via useRef. Depends on 012-003.

title: 012-005: Create useLiveClock hook
description: Create src/hooks/use-live-clock.ts returning current Date, re-renders once per minute aligned to the top of the minute via setTimeout+setInterval pattern. Cleans up on unmount. Returns current Date.

title: 012-004: Create useKioskData hook — filtered today and upcoming events
description: Create src/hooks/use-kiosk-data.ts accepting KioskConfig. Reads events and members from localStorage. Calls expandRecurringEvents. Filters by memberFilter (if non-empty). Returns { todayEvents: KioskEvent[], upcomingEvents: KioskEvent[] } for today and next 7 days respectively. Maps CalendarEvent to KioskEvent (resolve member color, sourceName='local'). Depends on 012-003.

title: 012-002: Add KioskConfig and KioskEvent types + storage key
description: In src/lib/types.ts add KioskConfig interface (memberFilter: string[], refreshIntervalMs: number default 300000) and KioskEvent interface (id, title, startTime?, endTime?, date, color, sourceName). In src/lib/storage.ts add kioskConfig storage key. TypeScript must compile with no errors.

title: 012-003: Create useKioskConfig hook
description: Create src/hooks/use-kiosk-config.ts exporting useKioskConfig() which uses useLocalStorage with default { memberFilter: [], refreshIntervalMs: 300000 }. Returns [config, setConfig]. Depends on 012-002.

title: 012-001: Add react-router-dom and wire up /kiosk route
description: Install react-router-dom (npm install react-router-dom). Wrap the app in a BrowserRouter in src/main.tsx. Refactor src/App.tsx so the existing calendar UI lives at route '/' and add a '/kiosk' route that renders a placeholder KioskPage component. Acceptance: navigating to /kiosk renders the placeholder without breaking the / route.

title: feat(013): Event Invitations & Multi-Platform Calendar Delivery
description:
## Summary
When events are created, updated, or cancelled, push invitations to family members in their own personal calendar apps — regardless of whether they use Google Calendar, Apple Calendar, Outlook, or any other iCalendar-compatible client.

## The Problem
ICS subscriptions are *passive pull* — family members subscribe and see events when they open their calendar app. But when a new event is created, no one gets proactively notified or gets the event added to their personal calendar. This feature adds *active push*.

## Key Features
- **iMIP (RFC 5546)** `METHOD:REQUEST` / `METHOD:CANCEL` email with `.ics` attachment — universally supported by GCal, Apple Calendar, Outlook, Thunderbird, etc.
- **Google Calendar deep-link** — "Add to Google Calendar" URL for GCal users (convenience, lower friction)
- **Per-member delivery preferences** — each family member stores: email address + preferred platform (Google / Apple / Outlook / Other)
- **SMTP transport** (configurable: host, port, credentials) for fully automatic sending; **`mailto:` fallback** for zero-infrastructure setups
- **Update propagation** — updated events send `METHOD:REQUEST` with incremented `SEQUENCE`
- **Cancellation** — deleted events send `METHOD:CANCEL` to remove event from recipients' calendars
- Organiser is excluded from their own invitation sends

## Spec
`specs/013-event-invitations-delivery/spec.md`

## Beads
family-calendar-7bs

title: feat(012): Kiosk / Family Display Mode
description:
## Summary
Fullscreen, read-only, auto-refreshing calendar view designed for a wall-mounted screen (TV, tablet, or monitor in a common area of the home).

## Key Features
- Route `/kiosk` renders a read-only fullscreen layout — no editing controls
- Live clock (updates every minute) + current date prominently displayed
- Today's events in large, legible text (readable from 2+ meters)
- Upcoming 7-day event list in a secondary panel
- Auto-refresh every 5 minutes (configurable); Page Visibility API triggers refresh on tab focus
- Landscape two-panel layout: today left, upcoming right
- Optional pre-configured family-member filter (P3)

## Spec
`specs/012-kiosk-display-mode/spec.md`

## Beads
family-calendar-gnm

title: 013: Event Invitations & Multi-Platform Calendar Delivery
description: Push invitations to family members in their personal calendar app when events are created/updated/cancelled. Mechanisms: iMIP (RFC 5546) METHOD:REQUEST/.ics email attachment (works with GCal, Apple Calendar, Outlook); GCal deep-link convenience URL for Google Calendar users; SMTP transport (configurable) with mailto: fallback. Per-member delivery preferences: email address + preferred platform. Update propagation via SEQUENCE; cancellation via METHOD:CANCEL. Solves the gap where ICS subscriptions are passive pull but event creation needs active push. Spec: specs/013-event-invitations-delivery/spec.md

title: 012: Kiosk / Family Display Mode
description: Fullscreen, read-only, auto-refreshing calendar view for a wall-mounted screen. Route: /kiosk. Shows live clock, today's events (large text), upcoming 7-day list. Auto-refresh every 5 min + Page Visibility API. Optional pre-configured member filter. No editing controls. Spec: specs/012-kiosk-display-mode/spec.md

title: need new github workflow that keeps beads in sync with issues on web
description: when i create issues in github, they should sync to beads

title: Refresh project README
description: Replace the stock Spark template README with project-specific setup, feature, and workflow documentation for the family-calendar app, then open and merge a PR.

title: feat(010): Week & Day Calendar Views with Time-Slot Grid
description:
## Summary
Add week view (7-column time-slot grid) and day view alongside the existing month view. View switcher control persistent across navigation and page reloads. Overlapping events shown side-by-side. All-day banner row.

**Spec**: `specs/010-week-day-views/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `010-week-day-views`  
**Priority**: P3 (Product)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: View the full week at a glance with time-slot grid; overlapping events visible
- **US2 (P2)**: View a single day in full time-slot detail
- **US3 (P3)**: View mode (month/week/day) is remembered on navigation and reload

## Key Requirements
- View switcher: Month / Week / Day toggle control
- Week view: 7 columns, hourly time rows, event blocks positioned by time and sized by duration
- Day view: single-day time-slot grid
- All-day events in a banner row at the top (not in timed grid)
- Overlapping events: side-by-side, not hidden
- Navigation shifts by correct unit (1 week in week, 1 day in day)
- Active view persisted in storage
- Member color-coding and filtering work identically in all views
- Mobile: narrow viewport falls back to 3-day or single-day view with swipe

## Out of scope for v1
Drag-to-move, drag-to-resize, multi-day event end-date field.

title: feat(011): Family User Profiles & Optional Self-Hosted PostgreSQL Backend
description:
## Summary
Family user accounts with login/logout. Shared calendar data, per-user preferences (filters, view). Optional PostgreSQL backend via `DATABASE_URL` env var for multi-device sync. localStorage fallback when no DB configured.

**Spec**: `specs/011-self-hosted-profiles/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `011-self-hosted-profiles`  
**Priority**: P3 (Product — roadmap)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Each family member has a personal login (username + password)
- **US2 (P2)**: Calendar data is shared across all accounts; preferences are per-account
- **US3 (P3)**: Optional PostgreSQL backend for multi-device sync (self-hosted deployment)

## Key Requirements
- User registration with username + hashed password (bcrypt or equivalent)
- Passwords NEVER stored in plaintext
- Sessions via secure HTTP-only cookies or signed tokens with expiry
- All calendar data (events, members, ICS subscriptions) shared across accounts
- Per-user preferences (view, filters) stored per account
- `DATABASE_URL` env var switches persistence from localStorage → PostgreSQL
- Without `DATABASE_URL`: full localStorage fallback (no regression)
- Database migration system for schema management
- Login screen as default entry point; unauthenticated routes redirect to login
- Docker Compose for local dev and self-hosted deployment

## ⚠️ Constitution Amendment Required
This feature requires amending **Principle IV** (Local-First Data Persistence). The amendment adds a conditional: local-first remains the default and mandatory fallback; server-backed mode is permitted when `DATABASE_URL` is explicitly configured.

## Out of scope for v1
Email-based password reset, multi-family/multi-tenant model.

title: feat(009): Event Attendance & RSVP per Family Member
description:
## Summary
Track per-member RSVP status (Going / Maybe / Not Going) per event. Show attendance summary on calendar grid event cards. Extend the existing member filter to support attendance-based filtering.

**Spec**: `specs/009-event-attendance-rsvp/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `009-event-attendance-rsvp`  
**Priority**: P2 (Product)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Mark attendance status (Going/Maybe/Not Going) for each assigned member per event
- **US2 (P2)**: See compact attendance summary on calendar grid event cards
- **US3 (P3)**: Filter calendar to show only events where a specific member is Going/Maybe

## Key Requirements
- `CalendarEvent.attendance` — optional map of `memberId → 'going' | 'maybe' | 'not-going' | 'unknown'`
- Attendance section in event detail view (when members are assigned)
- Changes persisted immediately via storage utility
- Calendar grid card: compact attendance indicator when status is set
- Member filter: optionally show only events where member is Going/Maybe
- Removing a member from an event deletes their attendance record

## Note
Attendance is set manually — no notification/invite system. Default for new members is `unknown` (not `going`).

title: feat(007): Recurring Events (daily/weekly/monthly/yearly with edit scope)
description:
## Summary
Add recurring event support — daily, weekly (selected days), monthly (day of month), and yearly patterns. Edit/delete single instance, this-and-following, or all events. End by date or after N occurrences.

**Spec**: `specs/007-recurring-events/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `007-recurring-events`  
**Priority**: P2 (Product)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Create a recurring event (e.g., weekly soccer practice)
- **US2 (P2)**: Edit or delete a single occurrence without affecting the series
- **US3 (P3)**: Set a recurrence end date or count

## Key Requirements
- Recurrence section in event creation/edit form
- Frequencies: Daily, Weekly (multi-day select), Monthly, Yearly
- End conditions: None (2-year horizon), end by date, end after N
- Edit scope dialog: "This event" / "This and following" / "All events"
- Modified occurrences visually distinguished from unmodified series
- Data model: `RecurrenceRule`, `seriesId`, `seriesExceptions`
- Serializable to localStorage — no circular refs
- ICS export (spec 001) stores recurrence as RRULE

## Note
Custom recurrence patterns (e.g., 3rd Tuesday of the month) are out of scope for v1.

title: feat(008): Event Locations with Map Link Integration
description:
## Summary
Add an optional free-text location field to events. Display with a map-pin icon in the event detail view. Clicking/tapping the location opens a mapping service. Include a location indicator on calendar grid event cards.

**Spec**: `specs/008-event-locations/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `008-event-locations`  
**Priority**: P2 (Product)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Add a location when creating or editing an event
- **US2 (P2)**: View location in event detail view and tap to get directions
- **US3 (P3)**: Location indicator (map pin icon) on calendar grid event cards

## Key Requirements
- Optional `location?: string` added to `CalendarEvent` type
- Location input in event creation/edit form
- Map-pin icon + clickable location in event detail view (opens maps via query string)
- Calendar grid event card shows pin icon when location is present
- Empty location = no field stored, no empty string
- ICS export uses standard `LOCATION` property (spec 001)
- No geocoding, autocomplete, or embedded map preview in v1

## Mapping URL pattern
`https://maps.google.com/?q={encodeURIComponent(location)}` — no API key required.

title: feat(005): GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
description:
## Summary
Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD: automated build + lint + test gate on all PRs, and a deploy workflow triggered on merges to `main`.

**Spec**: `specs/005-github-actions-cicd/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `005-github-actions-cicd`  
**Priority**: P1 (Platform — quality gate)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Every PR gets an automated build + lint quality gate; failing checks block merge
- **US2 (P2)**: Test suite runs automatically on every push (once spec 006 is implemented)
- **US3 (P3)**: Merges to `main` trigger an automatic deployment

## Key Requirements
- Workflow runs on `pull_request` (main) and `push` (main)
- Uses `npm ci` with Node 22 (from `.nvmrc` — depends on spec 004)
- Runs `lint` and `build`; fails job on errors
- Conditional test step (when `test` script exists)
- Deploy job only on `push` to `main`; no secrets in YAML
- All sensitive values use GitHub Actions secrets

## Dependencies
- Spec 004 (Node pinning) must be complete first
- Spec 006 (testing framework) activates the test step in CI

title: feat(006): Testing Framework & TDD Standards (Vitest + React Testing Library)
description:
## Summary
Install Vitest + React Testing Library. Establish TDD as the team standard. Add coverage gates (70% lines, 60% branches for `src/lib/`). Write initial tests for existing utilities.

**Spec**: `specs/006-testing-framework-tdd/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `006-testing-framework-tdd`  
**Priority**: P1 (Platform — quality gates)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Developer can write and run tests for calendar utilities in under 5 seconds
- **US2 (P2)**: Developer can write component tests with React Testing Library
- **US3 (P3)**: Coverage is tracked and enforced; CI fails below thresholds

## Key Requirements
- Vitest as test runner (zero-config with Vite)
- React Testing Library for component tests
- `test` and `test:coverage` npm scripts
- Coverage thresholds: 70% lines, 60% branches (`src/lib/`)
- Unit tests for all functions in `src/lib/calendar.ts` and `src/lib/utils.ts`
- Component test for `CalendarGrid`
- `localStorage` mocked in test environment
- TDD standard documented in CONTRIBUTING.md/README

## Note
**TDD standard**: tests MUST be written before implementation for all new utility functions and components going forward.

title: chore(004): Node.js Version Pinning & Upgrade to v22 LTS
description:
## Summary
Pin the project to Node.js 22 LTS. Node 20 reached end-of-life in April 2026 and is no longer receiving security patches.

**Spec**: `specs/004-node-version-upgrade/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `004-node-version-upgrade`  
**Priority**: P1 (Platform — security/maintenance)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: Project runs on Node 22 LTS without warnings or errors
- **US2 (P2)**: Node version is consistent across local, CI, and deployment environments

## Key Requirements
- Add `.nvmrc` pinning Node 22.x at the repository root
- Add `"engines": { "node": ">=22" }` to `package.json`
- Verify `npm run dev`, `build`, and `lint` pass on Node 22
- Update README/contributing docs with required Node version

## Dependency
Requires Node 22-compatible transitive dependencies — audit and update any that are not. This is also a prerequisite for CI/CD spec (005).

title: feat(003): Replace GitHub Spark KV with localStorage Backend
description:
## Summary
Remove the hard `@github/spark` `useKV` dependency and replace with a typed `localStorage` persistence utility, enabling the app to run in any environment.

**Spec**: `specs/003-local-storage-backend/spec.md`  
**Branch**: `003-local-storage-backend`  
**Priority**: P1 (Platform — prerequisite for portability)

## User Stories
- **US1 (P1)**: App runs without `@github/spark` installed; data persists across reloads
- **US2 (P2)**: Corrupted or missing storage is handled gracefully (resets to defaults)
- **US3 (P3)**: All storage access is centralized in a single typed utility module

## Key Requirements
- Remove `useKV` from `App.tsx`; replace with typed `get`/`set` storage functions in `src/lib/`
- No raw `JSON.parse`/`localStorage.setItem` outside the utility module
- Typed defaults when storage is missing or malformed
- `localStorage` exceptions (quota, access denied) caught without crashing the app
- All existing functionality (events, members, filtering) unchanged after migration

## Constitution
Implements **Principle IV** (Local-First Data Persistence) — currently blocked by Spark KV dependency.

## Labels
`platform` `p1` `no-backend`

title: 009: Event Attendance & RSVP
description: Track per-member RSVP status (Going/Maybe/Not Going) per event. Show attendance summary on calendar cards. Attendance filter.\nSpec: specs/009-event-attendance-rsvp/spec.md

title: 008: Event Locations
description: Add optional location field to events. Display with map-pin icon in detail view and calendar grid. Location link opens mapping service.\nSpec: specs/008-event-locations/spec.md

title: 007: Recurring Events
description: Add recurring event support with daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly repeat patterns. Edit/delete single instance or all-future. End by date or count.\nSpec: specs/007-recurring-events/spec.md

title: Phase 4: Merged ICS export with VALARM stripped (US2-P2)
description: Implement export UI: button in App header (or subscriptions sheet) that calls exportToIcsString(), strips all VALARM components, and triggers a .ics file download via Blob + URL.createObjectURL. Include a copyable code block for manual import. Acceptance: downloaded .ics contains all subscription events and zero VALARM components.

title: feat: ICS Calendar Subscriptions (001)
description: Track all implementation work for the ICS calendar subscriptions feature. Users can subscribe to external ICS URLs; events appear on the calendar grid. App also exports a merged, VALARM-stripped ICS file. Spec: specs/001-ics-calendar-subscriptions/spec.md | Plan: specs/001-ics-calendar-subscriptions/plan.md

title: 012-015: Responsive layout — tablet and portrait adaptation
description: Update KioskPage for smaller/portrait viewports: <1024px or portrait orientation switches to single-column stacked layout. No horizontal scroll at 1024x768 (SC-004). Text >=18px at all breakpoints. Use Tailwind lg: responsive prefixes. Depends on 012-012.

title: 012-014: Build KioskConfigSheet — settings panel for kiosk options
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskConfigSheet.tsx using Sheet UI. Multi-select family member filter (checkboxes), refresh interval input (in minutes). Save via useKioskConfig. Add 'Kiosk Settings' button to main app header in App.tsx. Depends on 012-003,012-013.

title: 012-011: Build KioskSyncWarning component
description: Create src/components/kiosk/KioskSyncWarning.tsx. Props: hasError: boolean. Renders only when hasError=true as an absolute-positioned bottom-right non-blocking warning badge with Warning icon + muted text. Depends on 012-001.

title: 011: Family User Profiles & Self-Hosted Backend
description: User accounts with login/logout. Shared calendar data, per-user preferences. Optional PostgreSQL backend for multi-device sync. Requires constitution amendment to Principle IV.\nSpec: specs/011-self-hosted-profiles/spec.md

title: 010: Week & Day Calendar Views
description: Add week view (7-column time-slot grid) and day view alongside month view. View switcher control. Overlapping event layout. View mode persisted.\nSpec: specs/010-week-day-views/spec.md

title: Phase 5: Subscription display customisation — edit name/color (US3-P3)
description: Add edit flow to IcsSubscriptionsSheet: allow updating name and color of an existing saved subscription. Confirm color is propagated consistently to grid events and filter chips. Acceptance: two subscriptions with different colors show distinctly on grid and in filters.
